calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Palley (via court call) for (petitioner) Berkley Peet. Douglas Bohne (via court call) for (beneficiary) Robert Peet.

The Court stated that there is a question regarding the fee of $13,458.00 that the petition seeks to pass to American Research Bureau (ARB) for an assignment to the heir, asked what information is available to prove that amount, that we need an indication of ARB's work on this case for this persons interest and asked if counsel had additional information to report. Mr. Palley stated that ARB does not keep track of their hours, that they keep a log as to what they did, that based on what he has seen is usually an accumulation of documents, that in this case it took some work due to the difference in the names (Pait/Peet) and that they have an extensive library. The Court stated that it needs to know what efforts were made in this case. Mr. Palley stated that ARB is paid on results and that they do not keep track of hours. The accounting petition was taken under submission to be considered on the evidence that the court has.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Oct-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Continued To Oct-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Palley (via court call) for Berkely Davis Peet. Doug Bohne for (beneficiary) Robert S. Peet.

The Court stated that it needs an itemization of the work done by the attorney and ARB, that there is a request for fees for 17 hours but no detail. Mr. Bohne reported that he thought that had been provided in the declaration, that ARB does not keep track of their hours, that they work on a contingency fee and that the attorneys fee comes out of their fee. The Court stated that it cannot award fees without documentation of what work was done. Mr. Bohne stated that they are paid on contingency, that the hours are now 22 and that he can provide details, confirmed that the court is requiring documentation of ARB who does not keep those records, that they are being paid for a result not hours spent, that the declaration states what they do their searches on and that the documentation they produced was provided to the administrator. The Court stated that it will need documentation of what was done in this case to determine what is reasonable. Mr. Bohne stated that they will have to figure out how to do this as they do not keep records. The accounting petition was continued to September 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Palley is to submit a revised order.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
